public.name: POWERINDIA
public.regime_lens: 🚦 RED — DEEPENING (mechanical derivation; operator card holds YELLOW, see §1.3): two RED-DEEPENS triggers that were watch-lines last week fired on the tape this week, and they are the operator's own enumerated triggers, not this agent's — silver lost the $58 escalation line with a weekly close of $55.695 (−7.1% WoW, week low $55.00), putting the $52 unconditional hard stop just ~$3.70 below versus ~$8 last week, and USDINR broke 96 (96.335 close, 96.33 web, fourth straight weakening session) firing the reduce-25 rail; a third trigger is at the line as gold broke below $4,000 (~$3,965-3,986, −3.8% WoW) while silver fell harder, expanding G:S to ~71-72 — the first weekly close AT the 72 hard gate, needing only one more to confirm the regime-shift signal; the week's analytical surprise is that June CPI printed SOFT (−0.4% m/m, 3.5% y/y vs 3.8% expected, core flat m/m at 2.6%) and metals crashed anyway, because the June collection window covers the June de-escalation while the July war re-flare is not in the print, so the market discounted the CPI as stale and priced the forward oil shock instead, driving hike odds UP to ~73% from ~66% and falsifying the 260712 assumption that a soft CPI was the relief path; Brent ran +11.1% WoW (cache close 76.25 -> 84.68; 88.10 on the 7/18 rail) on the US naval blockade of Iranian ports (reinstated 7/14), nine-plus ships attacked since 7/6 and Hormuz traffic down to eight vessels with transponders off, though the $105 oil gate is still ~$17-21 away; TNX remains FAILING at 4.541-4.569, oscillating across the 4.55 abort and keeping new-tranche deploys mechanically BLOCKED; the offsets are genuinely intact and are the reason this is a deepening rather than a cascade — CCC OAS 970bp benign through two war weeks, DXY drifting DOWN to 100.54-100.75 with ~1.3-1.5 of cushion, RBI reserves building a second week to $675.16B, India VIX 13.15 with no equity crack, and COT MM net +11,501 still un-crowded; the operative watch is now almost entirely downside — $52 is the number that matters, and the two questions that decide the next fortnight are whether Brent breaks above the > $105 gate and whether G:S posts a second close above 72 · base: Geopolitical Stagflation over Policy Transition, hawkish and re-tightening on a forward-looking oil impulse rather than on realised inflation → TIGHTENING · shock: IRAN/HORMUZ — ESCALATED FROM ACTIVE WAR OVERLAY TO NAVAL BLOCKADE: the US reinstated its blockade of Iranian ports on 7/14, formally ending the June 17 MoU détente; at least nine commercial ships attacked since 7/6; Hormuz commercial traffic collapsed to eight vessels on Thursday, the lowest in three weeks, with vessels running transponders dark; maritime risk assessed as "worst-case scenario"; the transmission channel has now BROADENED from last week's rates-and-risk-asset path into the oil complex itself (Brent +11.1% WoW), but remains ~$17-21 short of the $105 gate that would fire the full stagflation chain — a blockade is a structurally different and more persistent configuration than a strike-exchange, and it is the single variable most likely to decide the regime over the next fortnight · action: RED-DEEPENING = no new deployment anywhere, and the posture shifts from "wait" to "watch the stop... (refreshed 2026-07-19)
